title: pittosporum tenuifolium subsp. fasciculatum
intro: Pittosporum tenuifolium subsp. fasciculatum, commonly known as the Kohuhu or New Zealand laurel, is a plant species native to New Zealand. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits associated with Pittosporum tenuifolium subsp. fasciculatum include its potential to reduce inflammation, relieve digestive issues, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
